_note: Ready-to-insert interactive_image block for a motor neuron. Image: motor-neuron_illus.webp (keyed transparent + compressed from the founder's generated PNG, 1147x860). To ship: upload the webp to R2 via the books asset-upload route, put the returned URL in `src`, and add this block to the target biology page. Once published it powers both the inline 'Test yourself' Label Sprint and the book's Bio Deck. Hotspots authored + browser-verified against the image on 2026-07-13.
caption: 📸 Tap each dot to explore — or hit 'Test yourself' to play Label Sprint

hotspots.label: Dendrites
hotspots.detail: Short, branched projections that **receive** signals from other neurons and carry them **toward** the cell body.

hotspots.label: Cell body (soma)
hotspots.detail: Contains the nucleus and cytoplasm and integrates incoming signals. Also called the **cyton** or perikaryon.

hotspots.label: Nucleus
hotspots.detail: The control centre of the cell body, housing the neuron's DNA.

hotspots.label: Axon
hotspots.detail: The single long fibre that carries the nerve impulse **away** from the cell body.

hotspots.label: Myelin sheath
hotspots.detail: A fatty **insulating** layer around the axon that speeds up impulse conduction.

hotspots.label: Node of Ranvier
hotspots.detail: A gap between myelin segments where the impulse **jumps ahead** (saltatory conduction), making it faster.

hotspots.label: Schwann cell
hotspots.detail: The cell that wraps around the axon to form **one segment** of the myelin sheath (in the PNS).

hotspots.label: Axon terminals
hotspots.detail: The branched endings (synaptic knobs) that pass the signal to the **next** neuron or a muscle.

generation_prompt: A clean modern educational anatomy illustration of a motor neuron, shown as a labelled structural diagram. Show: cell body (soma), nucleus, dendrites, axon, myelin sheath, nodes of Ranvier, Schwann cell, axon terminals. Semi-realistic textbook style, muted palette, flat, solid deep-charcoal background #121316, no text/labels/numbers/leader lines. Landscape 4:3, high resolution.
